title: CEO Interview with James Regan of Oriole - Semiwiki
gist: Daniel Nenni interviews Oriole CEO James Regan, who argues the data-center network (not compute) is becoming the AI bottleneck; Oriole, spun out of UCL on 20+ years of optical research, has built a 'pure photonic' single-hop, contention-free AI network (PRISM and PRISM Ultra) that keeps data as light end-to-end to cut latency and power, framing tokens-per-second as directly tied to customer revenue.

insights.claim: Regan argues the network, not compute, is becoming the AI bottleneck because electrical switching architectures cannot keep up with modern AI scale and speed, especially for latency-bound inference.
insights.speaker: James Regan
insights.so_what: Reframes the AI scaling constraint from GPUs to interconnect; networking/photonics becomes the value layer to watch as inference dominates spend.
insights.quote: Electrical switching architectures simply can’t keep up with the scale and speed that modern AI demands.

insights.claim: Regan ties network latency directly to revenue: tokens per second and tokens per second per user are directly tied to revenue, so network delay makes GPUs wait, dropping throughput and immediately hitting the bottom line.
insights.speaker: James Regan
insights.so_what: Converts a technical metric into a P&L argument; if latency directly maps to revenue, network upgrades have a quantifiable ROI for inference operators.
insights.quote: tokens per second, and tokens per second per user, are directly tied to revenue.
insights.locator: That has very direct consequences, particularly for inference...

insights.claim: Regan contends the industry's default response of 'just add more' (more GPUs, switches, energy) runs into hard limits of power, cost, and complexity and does not scale indefinitely.
insights.speaker: James Regan
insights.so_what: The brute-force-scaling-has-limits view supports demand for architectural (not incremental) infra solutions; aligns with broader power-constraint themes.
insights.quote: if you want more performance, the industry response is essentially to just add more. More GPUs, more switches, more energy. But that only takes you so far.

insights.claim: Oriole claims to have built the world's first pure photonic AI network where data stays as light end-to-end, every node connects directly to every other node, and the network is single-hop, fully connected, and contention-free with no switching layers, queuing, or waiting.
insights.speaker: James Regan
insights.so_what: A single-hop contention-free topology is an architectural (not incremental) differentiator; if real and scalable, it disrupts electrical-switch incumbents.
insights.quote: data stays as light end-to-end... every node connects directly to every other node... the network operates in a single-hop, fully connected, contention-free topology
insights.locator: What does the competitive landscape look like...

insights.claim: Regan positions competitors as merely incremental (faster electrical switches, better optical links, hybrid scale-up/scale-out) all bound by the same underlying architecture, whereas Oriole removes that architecture's constraints entirely.
insights.speaker: James Regan
insights.so_what: The 'architectural vs incremental' framing is the core investment narrative; the risk is whether incumbents' incremental path is good-enough for most workloads.
insights.quote: Others are optimizing the existing system. We’re removing the constraints of that system entirely and building the system that comes after it.
insights.locator: What does the competitive landscape look like...

insights.claim: Oriole was spun out of University College London on more than 20 years of optical-networking research and built its full-stack system by starting from the problem of scaling AI and working backwards, rather than starting from a technology seeking an application.
insights.speaker: James Regan
insights.so_what: Deep-tech provenance and problem-first framing are de-risking signals for a frontier optical-networking startup; relevant for assessing technical credibility.
insights.quote: Oriole was founded out of University College London on the back of more than 20 years of research into optical networking

insights.claim: Oriole's PRISM (Photonic Routing Infrastructure for Scalable Models) is already up and running and being taken into real-world deployments, while PRISM Ultra (announced March) pushes light-in/light-out closer to the processor, dramatically increasing scale and performance with direct processor connectivity and one-hop communication across very large systems.
insights.speaker: James Regan
insights.so_what: PRISM being operational moves Oriole past pure R&D risk; PRISM Ultra's processor-proximity roadmap is the scale catalyst to track.
insights.quote: we’ve built PRISM (Photonic Routing Infrastructure for Scalable Models), the first and that’s already up and running today and we are now taking it into real-world deployments and scaling it
insights.locator: What new features/technology are you working on?

insights.claim: Oriole uses two engagement models: a transactional model for PRISM (data-center builders, partners, integrators) and a strategic, partnership-led model for PRISM Ultra engaging directly with hyperscalers and leading-edge AI-infrastructure builders.
insights.speaker: James Regan
insights.so_what: Hyperscaler-led design partnerships for PRISM Ultra are the high-value adoption channel; the staircase from demo to production deployment is the commercialization milestone to monitor.
insights.quote: For PRISM Ultra, it’s much more strategic and partnership-led, engaging directly with hyperscalers and leading-edge organisations who are building the next generation of AI infrastructure.
insights.locator: How do customers normally engage with your company?
